stimulus.content: Country A lets people own shops and set their own prices. Country B has the government decide what goods to make, how much to make, and what price to charge. Citizens in Country B cannot open their own stores.

stem: Which country has an economic system most like the American free enterprise system?

choices.text: Country A, because people control businesses
Country B, because the government sets prices
Country A, because the government owns all shops
Country B, because citizens pick what to make

answer_explanation: Country A is most like the American free enterprise system because individuals can own businesses and set their own prices. In contrast, Country B represents a command economy where the government makes all economic decisions. The American economy developed around the idea of free enterprise.
